name: Amitriptyline
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Amoxicillin
notes: Take with food; finish the full course

name: Amoxicillin-clavulanate (Augmentin)
notes: Take with food; finish the full course

name: Atropine eye drops
notes: 2 drops every 2 to 4 hours as needed for secretions

name: Azithromycin
notes: Take 2 hours after a meal

name: Baclofen
notes: Take with meals; do not discontinue abruptly

name: Benzydamine
notes: Rinse or gargle with 15 mL every 2 to 3 hours; do not swallow

name: Bisacodyl
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Buprenorphine
notes: Apply to clean, dry, non-hairy skin on upper arm or chest

name: Buprenorphine
notes: Put a tablet under the tongue when there is severe pain

name: Celecoxib
notes: Take with a meal or snack

name: Ciprofloxacin
notes: Do not take with dairy products or antacids; drink plenty of water

name: Clarithromycin
notes: Take with or without food

name: Clonazepam
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Clotrimazole
notes: Topical: Apply to fungal lesions

name: Dexamethasone
notes: Take in the morning with food to prevent insomnia and stomach upset

name: Diazepam
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Diclofenac
notes: Take with or immediately after food to protect the stomach

name: Docusate sodium
notes: Take with a full glass of water; swallow whole

name: Domperidone
notes: Take 15–30 minutes before meals

name: Donepezil
notes: Take at bedtime; can be taken with or without food

name: Doxycycline
notes: Take with a full glass of water; sit upright for 30 mins after to prevent reflux

name: Duloxetine
notes: Take at the same time each day; do not open or chew capsules

name: Escitalopram
notes: Take once daily in the morning or evening; with or without food

name: Esomeprazole
notes: Take at least 1 hour before breakfast; swallow whole

name: Famotidine
notes: Take 30 minutes before food

name: Fentanyl transdermal patch
notes: Apply to clean, flat skin; avoid exposing patch area to direct heat sources

name: Fluconazole
notes: Take with or without food

name: Fluoxetine
notes: Take in the morning with or without food

name: Fosfomycin
notes: Dissolve powder in cold water and drink immediately on an empty stomach

name: Furosemide
notes: Take in the morning to avoid waking up at night to urinate

name: Gabapentin
notes: Can cause drowsiness initially

name: Glycopyrronium bromide
notes: Look for urinary output

name: Granisetron
notes: Take with or without food

name: Haloperidol
notes: For agitation, delirium or vomiting

name: Haloperidol Syrup
notes: For agitation, delirium or vomiting

name: Ibuprofen
notes: Take with food

name: Itraconazole
notes: Take immediately after a full meal for optimal absorption

name: Lactulose
notes: Take with water or juice; may take 24–48 hours to see results

name: Levofloxacin
notes: Take with a full glass of water

name: Levocetirizine
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Liquid Paraffin + Milk of Magnesia + Sodium Picosulfate
notes: Can increase if there is no stools and decrease if stool frequency increases more than 4 per day

name: Loperamide
notes: Take 2 tablets initially, then 1 tablet after each loose bowel movement; maximum 8 tablets/day

name: Lorazepam
notes: Can be taken sublingually or orally for rapid relief of anxiety or breathlessness

name: Metoclopramide Syrup
notes: Take 5 to 10 mL for vomiting 30 minutes before food

name: Metoclopramide
notes: Take 30 minutes before meals; maintain at least 6 hours between doses

name: Metronidazole
notes: Topical: Apply to wound

name: Midazolam
notes: Administered nasally for dyspnoea or seizures

name: Mirtazapine
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Montelukast + Levocetirizine
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Morphine
notes: Take every 4 hours: 6 am, 10 am, 2 pm, 6 pm and double dose at 10 pm

name: Nebulisation Budecort
notes: Nebulisation for 20 minutes or until respule content is over

name: Nebulisation Levosalbutamol + Ipratropium
notes: Nebulisation for 20 minutes or until respule content is over

name: Nefopam
notes: Take with or without food; may color urine pink

name: Nitrofurantoin
notes: Take with food or milk to enhance absorption and reduce nausea

name: Olanzapine
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Omeprazole
notes: Take in the morning 30–60 minutes before breakfast; swallow whole

name: Ondansetron
notes: Take with or without food

name: Oxybutynin
notes: Take with or without food

name: Papain + Urea
notes: Topical: Apply to wound on yellowish slough

name: Pantoprazole
notes: Take in the morning 1 hour before food; swallow whole

name: Paracetamol
notes: Take every 8 or 6 hours as needed; do not exceed 4g in 24 hours

name: Pramipexole
notes: Take with or without food; may cause sudden sleep onset

name: Prednisolone
notes: Take in the morning with breakfast to reduce insomnia/stomach upset

name: Pregabalin
notes: Take at the same times each day; can cause dizziness/drowsiness initially

name: Quetiapine
notes: Watch out for excessive daytime sleepiness

name: Risperidone
notes: Take at bedtime; monitor for extrapyramidal side effects

name: Sertraline
notes: Take once daily in morning with or without food

name: Sodium valproate
notes: Take with or immediately after food

name: Spironolactone

name: Sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim (Co-trimoxazole)
notes: Take with a full glass of water; can be with or without food

name: Tolvaptan

name: Tramadol + Paracetamol
notes: Take every 8 hourly or 6 hourly as needed for pain

name: Tramadol
notes: Take every 8 hourly or 6 hourly as needed for pain

name: Tranexamic acid
notes: Take three times daily for bleeding management; swallow whole

name: Trazodone
notes: Take at bedtime

name: Zoledronic acid
notes: Administered as an intravenous/subcutaneous infusion over no less than 15 minutes

name: Zolpidem
notes: Take at bedtime
